title: Add Trust negative scenario suite

sources.reason: Defines the ten required negative scenario families.
Existing fail-closed guard functions under test.

riskAssessment.reason: The suite exercises existing deterministic guards without changing runtime policy.

executionDecision.reason: User explicitly authorized the sequential Trust Layer execution plan.

preReviewWarnings: Every blocked scenario must assert a fail-closed machine-readable result.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: At least ten negative scenario families are automated.
Blocked scenarios assert fail-closed results and safe alternatives where applicable.
The negative scenario suite passes in the full quality run.
Scenario evidence is machine-readable and tied to the tests.
guidelines: Negative scenarios must assert fail-closed outcomes.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: Trust guard behavior needs a single regression suite proving that dangerous, ambiguous, bypass, forged, and scope-expanding requests cannot pass silently.
intent.rationale: A consolidated negative suite protects the fail-closed boundary as later Trust Layer work changes presentation and release behavior.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
